description:
West Yellowstone fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and multiple West Yellowstone topic files: The year-round population figure is stale. The site uses 1,272 residents, but Grokipedia's West Yellowstone page reports 1,202 as of 2024.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: West Yellowstone hiking metadata is stale. The meta description says 20 trailheads, while the current hiking page body uses 13 trailheads within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: West Yellowstone fishing metadata is stale. The meta description says 37 lakes, while the current fishing page uses 10 lakes within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's West Yellowstone, Montana page and internal West Yellowstone page comparisons across hub, jobs, cost-of-living, weekend itinerary, hiking, and fishing copy.
title: Fact-check West Yellowstone pages

description:
Butte fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Butte hiking metadata is stale. The meta description says 25 trailheads, while the current hiking page body and FAQ use 4 trailheads within 30 miles.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Butte fishing metadata is stale. The fishing meta description and guide description still say 69 mountain lakes, while the current fishing page body uses 13 lakes within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/topics/butte/Hiking.tsx versus web/components/town/topics/butte/WeekendItinerary.tsx: Discovery Ski Area distance is internally inconsistent. The hiking page uses 38 miles, while the weekend itinerary says 25 miles west near Anaconda.

Evidence checked during review: internal Butte page comparisons and Grokipedia-backed regional searches for Discovery Ski Area context.
title: Fact-check Butte pages

description:
Kalispell fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/topics/kalispell/Schools.tsx: The library system name is wrong. The page says "Imaginary Nest Public Library," but the Flathead Valley public library system is ImagineIF Libraries.

2. web/components/town/topics/kalispell/CostOfLiving.tsx: The population sentence conflates the city and the county. It says Kalispell has "a population of roughly 29,886 in Flathead County," which reads as though 29,886 is the county population. That figure is being used for Kalispell itself, while Flathead County is much larger.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Kalispell fishing metadata is internally inconsistent. The fishing meta description says 11 access sites, while the guide description and the outdoor-recreation FAQ use 10 public access sites within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: internal Kalispell page comparisons, Grokipedia-backed Kalispell searches, and the named Flathead Valley institutions referenced in the current copy.
title: Fact-check Kalispell pages

description:
Billings fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Billings school metadata appears stale. The meta description says Billings Public Schools has 16,000 students, but Grokipedia's Billings Public Schools coverage reports 16,570 students across the district.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Billings fishing metadata is stale relative to the current 30-mile content. The fishing meta description and guide description still say 20 fishing access sites, while the hub intro, FAQ, and fishing page body use 8 fishing access sites within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and related Billings cost-of-living copy: Billings is described as having the most affordable housing of any major Montana city, but the site also gives Great Falls a lower affordability ratio (5.1 vs Billings at 5.4). That superlative should be corrected or removed.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Billings Public Schools coverage, internal Billings page comparisons, and cross-checks against other site affordability figures.
title: Fact-check Billings pages

close_reason: Added shared map coverage to all town topic page types and moved hub-page recreation next to the map for easier exploration.
description: Add the shared map section to all town topic page types and move the town hub recreation section closer to the map so map exploration and recreation browsing stay near each other.
title: Expand maps across topic pages

close_reason: Corrected the Missoula schools, hiking, fishing, and metadata issues identified during fact-check review.
description:
Missoula fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/topics/Schools.tsx: MCPS structure is wrong. The page says the district has only two middle schools (Washington and Meadow Hill) and frames Big Sky High as being in the surrounding area. Official MCPS sources show C.S. Porter Middle School is also an MCPS middle school, and Big Sky High School is an MCPS grades 9-12 high school in Missoula.

2. web/components/town/topics/Schools.tsx: The Missoula Public Library opening year is wrong. The page says the new building opened in 2022, but the new library opened and celebrated its grand opening in 2021.

3. web/components/town/topics/Hiking.tsx: The Bob Marshall Wilderness proximity claim is overstated. Saying the complex is reachable within about an hour's drive from Missoula appears too aggressive; official Lolo National Forest access information places the Seeley Lake access area about 60 miles northeast of Missoula, with wilderness trailheads farther beyond that.

4. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Missoula hiking metadata is stale. The Missoula hiking meta description says 90 trailheads near Missoula, while the page body and current 30-mile content use 49 trailheads within 30 miles.

5. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Missoula fishing metadata is stale. The Missoula fishing meta description says 44 fishing access sites, while the page body and FAQ use 28 fishing access sites within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: official MCPS school pages for Big Sky High and C.S. Porter Middle School, official UM enrollment pages, official Destination Missoula fishing page, authoritative Missoula Public Library opening coverage, and Lolo National Forest access information.
title: Fact-check Missoula pages

description:
Red Lodge fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and multiple Red Lodge topic files: The population figure is stale. The site repeatedly uses 2,399 residents, but Grokipedia's Red Lodge page reports a population of approximately 2,700 as of 2024.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Red Lodge fishing metadata is stale. The meta description says 23 fishing access sites and 255 lakes, while the current fishing page body and FAQ use 15 fishing access sites and 149 lakes within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Red Lodge hiking metadata is slightly stale. The meta description says 13 trailheads, while the current hiking page body uses 12 trailheads within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Red Lodge, Montana page and internal Red Lodge page comparisons across hub, cost-of-living, housing, weekend itinerary, hiking, fishing, and FAQ copy.
title: Fact-check Red Lodge pages

description:

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Anaconda fishing metadata is stale. The meta description says 86 lakes and 7 access sites, while the current fishing page body uses 48 lakes and 2 fishing access sites within 30 miles.

title: Fact-check Anaconda pages

description:
Columbia Falls fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and related Columbia Falls/Whitefish hub copy: The site's Glacier-gateway superlatives are internally contradictory. Columbia Falls says it is 17 miles from Glacier's west entrance and therefore the most convenient gateway town / closer than Whitefish, while the Whitefish hub also says Whitefish is 17 miles from the west entrance and the closest town of any size to the park. Those claims cannot all be true as written and should be reconciled.

Evidence checked during review: internal Columbia Falls and Whitefish hub/topic comparisons.
title: Fact-check Columbia Falls pages

description:
Livingston fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and multiple Livingston topic files: Yellowstone's north-entrance distance is too short. The site repeatedly says Livingston is 44 miles from Gardiner / Yellowstone's north entrance, but Grokipedia places Pray about 30 miles south of Livingston and 30 miles north of Yellowstone's north entrance, which points to a route distance closer to about 60 miles from Livingston.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Livingston fishing metadata is stale. The fishing meta description still says 145 mountain lakes, while the current guide description and Livingston fishing page use 40 mountain lakes within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Pray, Montana page, Grokipedia-backed Livingston searches, and internal Livingston page comparisons across hub, cost-of-living, hiking, fishing, and FAQ copy.
title: Fact-check Livingston pages

description:
Great Falls fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and web/components/town/topics/great-falls/CostOfLiving.tsx: Great Falls is repeatedly described as the most affordable major Montana city, but the site's own Butte pages use a lower affordability ratio (Butte 4.7 vs Great Falls 5.1). That superlative should be corrected or removed.

Evidence checked during review: internal cross-checks between Great Falls and Butte affordability claims, plus Grokipedia-backed Great Falls searches for surrounding landmark context.
title: Fact-check Great Falls pages

description:

Evidence checked during review: internal Whitefish page comparisons and Grokipedia-backed Whitefish searches for broader corroboration.
title: Fact-check Whitefish pages

description:
Helena fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/topics/helena/Schools.tsx: The page says Carroll College is Helena's sole institution of higher education. Grokipedia also documents Helena College University of Montana in Helena, so that statement is false.

2. web/components/town/topics/helena/Fishing.tsx and web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Helena's Missouri-at-Craig distance is too short. The fishing page and FAQ use 32 miles, but Grokipedia places Craig about 43 miles north of Helena.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Helena's hub intro says Helena has the most affordable housing of any major Montana city, but the site's own affordability figures give Helena a 6.6 ratio while Billings is described elsewhere at 5.4. That superlative should be corrected or removed.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Helena College University of Montana page, Grokipedia's Craig and Gates of the Mountains pages, and internal cross-checks against other site affordability claims.
title: Fact-check Helena pages

description:
Hamilton fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts versus web/components/town/topics/hamilton/Schools.tsx and web/components/town/topics/hamilton/Hiking.tsx: Hamilton's nearby ski-area distances are internally inconsistent. The FAQ says Lost Trail Powder Mountain is 40 miles south and Discovery Ski Area is 43 miles east, while the schools and hiking pages say Lost Trail is 62 miles south and Discovery is 59 miles east. The site should use one verified mileage/routing convention.

Evidence checked during review: internal Hamilton page comparisons plus Grokipedia-backed Bitterroot/Lost Trail regional searches for geographic context.
title: Fact-check Hamilton pages

description:
Bozeman fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Bozeman's Yellowstone north-entrance distance is wrong in the hub intro and FAQ. The current copy says Yellowstone's north entrance at Gardiner is 48 miles from Bozeman, but another Bozeman page already says the north entrance is roughly 80 miles south, and external route references place the drive at about 78 to 80 miles.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and web/components/town/topics/bozeman/WeekendItinerary.tsx: Bridger Bowl distance is inconsistent and too short in some Bozeman copy. Grokipedia describes Bridger Bowl as approximately 16 miles northeast of Bozeman, but the hub FAQ and weekend itinerary use 12 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: The outdoor-recreation FAQ contains an internal contradiction. It says Bozeman has 1 ski area within 30 miles, then names both Bridger Bowl and Big Sky in the same parenthetical. That should be reconciled, especially because other Bozeman copy places Big Sky much farther away.

4. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts versus web/components/town/topics/bozeman/Jobs.tsx and web/components/town/topics/bozeman/WeekendItinerary.tsx: Big Sky distance is internally inconsistent across Bozeman pages. Some copy says 32 miles from Bozeman, while the jobs page says 45 miles southwest. The site should use one verified distance/routing convention.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Bridger Bowl page, Grokipedia-backed Bozeman recreation searches, internal Bozeman page comparisons, and route references for Bozeman to Gardiner.
title: Fact-check Bozeman pages

description:

description:
Big Sky fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Big Sky hiking metadata is stale. The meta description says 42 trailheads, while the current hiking page body uses 23 trailheads within 30 miles.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Big Sky fishing metadata is stale. The meta description says 75 lakes, while the current fishing page uses 18 lakes within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and related Big Sky copy: The hub intro says Big Sky is roughly midway between Bozeman and Yellowstone's north entrance, but the site's own Bozeman audit already established Gardiner / Yellowstone's north entrance is much farther from Bozeman than Big Sky is. That midpoint framing should be verified or removed.

Evidence checked during review: internal Big Sky page comparisons, cross-checks against the earlier Bozeman Yellowstone-distance audit, and Grokipedia-backed regional searches.
title: Fact-check Big Sky pages
